llm_explanation: Moving the king to c2 centralizes it and supports the advancement of the c-pawn, which is crucial in this simplified endgame. This move also keeps the king close to the pawn, helping to escort it towards promotion while maintaining control over key squares.

The best move is to advance the white king from c6 to c7. This move supports the promotion of the c-pawn on the next move. Black's king on e2 is too far to stop the pawn, and the white king is effectively blocking the black king from approaching the pawn. White will promote the pawn to a queen on the next move, securing a winning advantage.

White should play e4 to start advancing the pawn towards promotion. The black king is too far to stop the pawn, and White's king is well placed to support the pawn's advance. This move initiates a winning plan for White.

White should push the pawn to h4 to start advancing it towards promotion. Black's king is too far to stop the pawn, and White's king is well placed to support the pawn's advance. This move initiates a winning plan for White.

White should play b3 to prevent Black's king from advancing further into White's territory. This move also prepares to support the b-pawn's advance and restricts Black's king movement, aiming to create a passed pawn or gain a winning endgame advantage.

White should play f3 to prevent Black's king from approaching the white king and pawns. This move helps White maintain control and prepares to advance the pawn safely, increasing the chances of promoting it.
